original: textify(a||(a=(0,f.Z)(["The Colt XLR mounts its boarding ramp to the front of the vessel. When deployed, it lowers on hydraulic supports into the shade of the angular prow. Like most vessels manufactured by Pyrite Industries, the ship’s design language is industrial. Huge up and down arrows stamp the ramp’s controls. Safety-orange support beams flank the access corridors, powder-coated with P.I.’s patented pigments and rated to withstand any kind of acid the PH scale can throw at it. You can exit the ship here. Ample cargo holds flank the ramp to the east and west to allow for quick access on and off the job site.The helm lies up a ramp to the south."])))

textify(n||(n=(0,f.Z)(["The port cargo hold stretches off to the north, lined with lockers for you to store your prized possessions in. There’s enough space to load up some pallets if need be, but you’re out to be a Rusher, not a trader - for now. The Colt XLR offers more space to the north, should you need it, and the boarding ramp sits just east of here, perfect for quick loading and unloading."])))

textify(i||(i=(0,f.Z)(["Most vessels have a single central hold, but the Colt XLR has two, one on each side of the center power generator. Embedded utility lights bathe everything in bright white illumination - even the stripes of neon-hued paint used to mark overhead girders and bulkhead rims. Plenty of footlockers and their bigger brothers hang from the port wall, making this the perfect place to access your vessel’s storage.\n\n\t\tThe ship’s nose lies to the north. The cargo hold stretches south."])))

textify(s||(s=(0,f.Z)(["Hatchways for upgrades to the ship (or rooms for your crew) line this passageway through the fore of the Colt. The corridor itself leads east around the ship’s secondary power generation systems, colored in garish safety orange and black. A cargo hold lies just to the south of here, matched by an identical one on the starboard side."])))

textify(r||(r=(0,f.Z)(["At the very fore of the Colt XLR, this narrow tunnel stretches between potential upgrade emplacements and crew quarters. When kitted for pure combat, there’s little reason to be here, captains that favor a robust crew will find themselves coming up here to visit their crew’s quarters - after confirming their location on the security console far to the ship’s aft. The tunnel at the XLR’s nose leads east and west."])))

textify(u||(u=(0,f.Z)(["Spaciousness isn’t a feature of the Colt’s nose section. The corridor is actually quite cramped, narrowed down to make room for the vessel’s sexy, angular look - and modular spaces for upgrades or additional crew quarters.\n\n\t\tThe nose section stretches west, though you can access your storage to the south."])))

textify(l||(l=(0,f.Z)(["The Colt’s cargo hold is long enough to handle a few pallets of merchandise. Hooks in the floor, wall, and ceiling sit ready to tie down anything you bring onboard, no matter the shape or size. More relevant to you are the footlockers lining the starboard wall, ready to store any excess equipment you’ve found on your journeys. The hold stretches south, though it narrows to the north around spaces for upgrades and crew quarters."])))

textify(h||(h=(0,f.Z)(["The starboard storage hold stretches out to the north from here, wrapping around the auxiliary power generation systems in the center of the vessel. Lined with crates and lockers, this is where you would want to store any excess loot you find on your adventures. You can also reach the boarding ramp just to the west for quick loading and unloading."])))

textify(c||(c=(0,f.Z)(["Like an ancient sports car, this vessel places its helm in the center of its considerable, somewhat-aerodynamic body. Transparent titaluminum alloy viewscreens provide nearly 180 degrees of horizontal viewing, and projected images above and below the pilot’s chair allow the Captain a perfect view of as much of the starscape as [pc.heShe] cares to witness. Of course, Pyrite’s control systems make use of old-fashioned buttons, switches, and levers. They may not be as flashy as the ship’s optics, but they’ll work in almost any situation - a feature appreciated by scrappy mechanics the galaxy over. You can use the helm to fly to a new destination. Just to the north is the craft’s boarding ramp, while the security console lies south, down a small ramp to the aft."])))

textify(d||(d=(0,f.Z)(["Pyrite’s idea of a security console is more of a security wall. Instead of a holographic, projected map, it relies on displaying information on a two-dimensional screen on the wall. It defaults to a map view with green dots to communicate life signs. Green labels note the designations of crew members while orange ones reveal potential interlopers. A detachable keyboard offers control over various display modes, should you need a view along a different axis or security sweeps on specific spectrums. You can use the security console to locate and interact with your crew. A space for a workshop lies to the east, and the Captain’s quarters sit just to the west."])))

textify(g||(g=(0,f.Z)(["Pyrite vessels aren’t built for comfort, but that doesn’t mean this ship’s relatively spartan Captain’s quarters is uncomfortable - far from it. The fold-away bed comes with a firm mattress and a 99.9% fluid resilience rating. It can shed semen, milk, and motor oil alike! A closet with pegboards and rotating storage cabinets allows you to hang up your clothing when not in use and securely pack away the rest of your kit. It even manages to squeeze in a bathroom with a full-size combination shower-tub. None of it looks fashionable, but it works and works well.\n\n\t\tThe security station lies east of here while the port-side engine access sits just to the south."])))

textify(p||(p=(0,f.Z)(["One of the Colt XLR’s sizable engines borders this dead-end corridor. Five danger-striped hatches offer access to maintenance tunnels into the works. With enough know-how, a mechanic could access any conceivable propulsion system without the need for dry-dock - assuming they don’t mind crawling between active plasma conduits and fuel lines.\n\n\t\tThe Captain’s quarters are just north of here."])))

textify(y||(y=(0,f.Z)(["True to its origins as a salvage and construction vessel, the Colt XLR comes with a built-in space for a workshop. Between the high voltage power sockets, the coolant quick-connects, and the waste vacuums, this space is the perfect place to install any interesting utility devices you find in your adventures. Security is directly west. Maintenance access to the vessel’s considerable engine housings leads south."])))

textify(m||(m=(0,f.Z)(["The starboard engine borders the eastern wall, covered in maintenance panels, lubricant caps, and anything else a mechanic would need access to in a pinch. For the inaccessible internals, small hatchways lead into the engine’s rumbling guts. So long as you have a mechanic without a hint of claustrophobia, nothing can ground this bird!\n\n\t\tJust to the north is a workshop space."])))
